page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical AMD EPYC 7B13 cores with multithreading enabled. The instance is configured with 32 GB of system memory, yielding an 8 GB per vCPU ratio, and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core scaling is limited, as shown by weak ratings in multi-core stress-ng, Geekbench multi-core, and software compilation tests. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is average, while small-block cached read bandwidth is relatively weak. Cryptographic and integer operations perform at average levels, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instruction sets.

The instance provides a cost-efficient entry point for memory-bound workloads that do not require high parallel processing power. Due to the weak multi-core performance and lower database operations scores, it is less suited for heavy multi-threaded compute or high-throughput database engines. It is best utilized for memory-intensive single-threaded applications, light caching layers, text processing, or small-scale web serving where memory capacity is prioritized over raw multi-core compute density.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. It offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core, with no bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core CPU execution, though multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth for small cached blocks are relatively weak. The instance is equipped with hardware-accelerated cryptography instructions like AES and SHA. Qualitatively, it represents a cost-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand heavy parallel processing. It is best suited for memory-bound applications, text processing, and light caching layers where memory capacity is the primary requirement.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM. It features AMD EPYC 7B13 processors, strong single-core performance, and no local storage.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring strong single-core performance and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for memory-bound workloads.
